2606.06056.title: Metamorphic Testing with the Rashomon Set: Explanation Faithfulness in Machine Learning
2606.06056.paper_summary: Multiple machine learning models can achieve near-equivalent predictive performance on the same task, yet provide divergent feature-based explanations. This is called the Rashomon effect of (explainable) machine learning, and it raises the question of which explanations, if any, are trustworthy. We propose a framework based on metamorphic testing that assesses explanation faithfulness without requiring ground-truth labels by exploring attributed feature importance from post-hoc explanation methods. Five metamorphic relations formalize expected consistency properties between model behavior and feature attributions. We apply this general framework to two tabular regression datasets and two post-hoc explainers (SHAP and LIME) to demonstrate the approach. The framework offers a practical, model-agnostic tool for selecting accurate models with reliable and trustworthy explanations.
2606.06056.paper_summary_zh: 多個機器學習模型在相同任務上可以達到近乎等效的預測性能，但卻提供不同的基於特徵的解釋。這被稱為（可解釋的）機器學習的拉肖門效應，並引發了哪些解釋（如果有的話）是值得信賴的問題。我們提出了一個基於變形測試的框架，該框架通過探索後驗解釋方法中的特徵重要性來評估解釋的真實性，而不需要真實標籤。五個變形關係形式化了模型行為和特徵歸因之間的預期一致性特性。我們將這一通用框架應用於兩個表格回歸數據集和兩個後驗解釋器（SHAP和LIME），以展示該方法。該框架提供了一個實用的、與模型無關的工具，用於選擇具有可靠和可信解釋的準確模型。
2606.06056.author: Helge Spieker et.al.
2606.06056.authors: Helge Spieker, Jørn Eirik Betten, Arnaud Gotlieb
